Forbidden.description: Credentials are valid but lack the scope required for this route, or the principal lacks membership in the target project.

NotFound.description: Resource not found OR not accessible to this principal. Leak parity: the response is identical in both cases so an attacker cannot enumerate resources via 404-vs-403 timing.

RateLimited.description: Per-bucket rate limit exceeded. The response carries ``Retry-After`` and the standard ``X-RateLimit-*`` headers (Limit / Remaining / Reset).

Unauthenticated.description: No credentials, malformed credentials, or revoked credentials. The envelope is leak-parity (same shape across all 401 paths) so an attacker cannot distinguish revoked vs. unknown via the body.

Unprocessable.description: Request body failed validation. For delta routes the response carries ``error``, ``delta_ordinal``, ``delta_type``, ``field``, ``reason``, ``recoverable``, and ``recovery_hint`` so a client can auto-recover or surface a typed error.

VersionConflict.description: Optimistic concurrency check failed — the branch's current version is not the one the client sent in ``expected_version``. The response carries ``current_version`` so the client can refetch + retry.

DeleteNodeDelta.description:
Cascade-delete a node + its descendant subtree.

Inventory § delete_node.
DeleteNodeDelta.title: DeleteNodeDelta

Edge.description:
Edge between two nodes' ports. Inventory § add_edge wire shape.

Wire contract: `sourceHandle` / `targetHandle` only (H2o-audit
Cluster F dropped the snake_case aliases). The Field aliases
declare the wire names; `populate_by_name=False` (the default)
means clients sending `source_handle` / `target_handle` in JSON
get the keys ignored via extra="ignore" — same observable
behavior as legacy edges.py:72-79 which only reads camelCase keys.
Tightening this (rejecting the snake_case keys outright) is a
separate decision; for parity with legacy we silently drop them.
Edge.title: Edge

FlattenBoundaryDelta.description:
Flatten a boundary: delete + promote children to its parent.

Inventory § flatten_boundary.
FlattenBoundaryDelta.title: FlattenBoundaryDelta

Node.description: Behavior or boundary node. Inventory § add_node wire shape.
Node.title: Node

NodeData.description:
Node data payload — variant by kind + is_external.

Designed for key-presence semantics: update_node_data uses
`.model_fields_set` to distinguish "the client omitted this key
(preserve)" from "the client sent null (set to null)". A field with
`default=None` looks identical post-validation; `model_fields_set`
is the only signal.

All fields are optional at the type level. Cross-field rules
(behavior nodes reject user_kind, external_kind required when
is_external=True, etc.) are enforced in validation_core.py — type
parsing here stays permissive so a partial update isn't blocked
by "missing required field" errors that don't apply to partials.
NodeData.title: NodeData

ReparentNodeDelta.description:
Move a node to a new parent (or top-level). H2o-audit Cluster A2.

`parent_id` key MUST be present on the wire — no key is rejected so
a client bug surfaces as a clean parse error rather than an
ambiguous no-op.
ReparentNodeDelta.title: ReparentNodeDelta

UpdateNodeDataDelta.description:
Update a node's data. Key-presence partial semantics.

Inventory § update_node_data. `after` may be empty; presence of a
field in `after.model_fields_set` distinguishes "set" from "omit".
UpdateNodeDataDelta.title: UpdateNodeDataDelta

V1CreateBranchBody.description:
Body for ``POST /v1/projects/{pid}/branches``. ``name`` defaults to the
next ``main-work[-N]`` for the caller, so a bodyless POST works.
V1CreateBranchBody.title: V1CreateBranchBody

deltas.description: Ordered list of delta operations to apply. Each must carry a `type` field in the closed write-surface vocabulary (add_node / delete_node / update_node_data / reparent_node / add_edge / delete_edge / flatten_boundary). Empty list returns 200 with `applied: false` and no version bump.
deltas.title: Deltas

expected_version.description: The branch.version the client believes is current. If it doesn't match the server's view at commit time, the route 409s with the actual current_version in the detail.
expected_version.title: Expected Version

positions.description: Optional explicit cell pins as `node_id -> {x, y}`, applied to the branch owner's view AFTER the deltas. **Additive**: a pin is introduced only for a node the owner hasn't already placed — it never overwrites a hand-placement and never deletes. Use only to override the derived grid placement for nodes you create.
positions.title: Positions

V1NodePosition.description: A single explicit pin coordinate on the `/v1/` write surface.
V1NodePosition.title: V1NodePosition

Verification.description: Test spec on a behavior node. Inventory § Sub-Records (Rule 12).
Verification.title: Verification

info.description:
The hydrate.sh public API. All routes are scoped to the `/v1/` prefix and require a Bearer API key (see Authentication).

**Stability:** `v1` is the wire-stable surface. Breaking changes will rebase to `/v2/`.
info.title: hydrate.sh v1 API

get.description: Returns `{"ok": true}` when the /v1/ surface is serving traffic. Unauthenticated. Use this for load-balancer and uptime-monitor checks; a 404 from this path means the /v1/ surface is disabled on the responding instance, not that the server is down.

post.description: Apply an ordered batch of structural changes to a branch under optimistic concurrency. Pass the `version` from the branch read as `expected_version`; a 409 with `current_version` is returned if the branch moved underneath you. Pair with the `Idempotency-Key` header for at-most-once apply across retries.

schema.title: Response Apply Branch Deltas V1 Branches  Branch Id  Deltas Post

post.summary: Apply a delta batch to a branch (OCC)

get.description: Read the full graph (nodes, ports, edges, version) of a specific branch. Use the returned `version` as `expected_version` on a subsequent apply to that branch.

schema.title: Response Fetch Branch Graph V1 Branches  Branch Id  Graph Get

post.description: Run the same validation pipeline as the branch apply, but roll back before commit. Returns 200 on success (with the projected version) or a structured 422 identifying the first failing delta. Use it before applying a batch you can't recover from.

schema.title: Response Validate Branch Deltas V1 Branches  Branch Id  Validate Post

post.summary: Dry-run a delta batch on a branch

get.description: Returns the active main-branch graph (nodes, ports, edges, version) for the project. Read-only: the main branch is merge-only. To make changes, fork a branch (`POST /v1/projects/{project_id}/branches`) and apply deltas to it.

parameters.description: The project whose main-branch graph to fetch.
parameters.name: project_id

schema.description: The project whose main-branch graph to fetch.
schema.title: Project Id
Response Fetch Graph V1 Graph  Project Id  Get

get.summary: Get the canonical graph for a project

get.description: Returns the boundary view for a single node: the inputs and outputs that cross the node's enforcement perimeter. Used by clients that visualize a node's contract surface in isolation from the rest of the graph.

parameters.description: The project the boundary lives in.
parameters.name: project_id

schema.description: The project the boundary lives in.
schema.title: Project Id
Response Fetch Boundary V1 Graph  Project Id  Boundary  Node Id  Get

get.summary: Get the boundary topology around a node

get.description: Returns a single node along with its directly connected neighbors and edges (the 1-hop topology). The node must belong to the project's active main branch.

parameters.description: The project the node lives in.
parameters.name: project_id

schema.description: The project the node lives in.
schema.title: Project Id
Response Fetch Node With Neighbors V1 Graph  Project Id  Node  Node Id  Get

get.summary: Get a node with its 1-hop neighborhood

get.description: Returns the projects the caller can see. For API-key callers the list is further filtered by the per-key project allowlist (if configured). Archived projects are excluded. An empty list is a normal response when the caller has no visible projects.

parameters.description: Maximum projects to return. Defaults to 100; max 200.
parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum projects to return. Defaults to 100; max 200.
schema.title: Limit
Response List Projects V1 Projects Get

get.description: List your active working branches on this project. The protected main branch is not included. Each branch is a unit of work you can read and apply changes to before merging into main.

parameters.description: The project to list branches for.
parameters.name: project_id

schema.description: The project to list branches for.
schema.title: Project Id
Response List Branches V1 Projects  Project Id  Branches Get

get.summary: List the caller's working branches on a project

post.description: Fork a new working branch from the project's main branch. The branch is copied from main's current state and owned by you; your changes stay isolated on it until merged. Requires an editor role, and there is a per-project limit on how many active branches you can hold at once.

parameters.description: The project to fork a branch in.
parameters.name: project_id

schema.description: The project to fork a branch in.
schema.title: Project Id
Response Create Branch V1 Projects  Project Id  Branches Post

post.summary: Start a unit of work (fork a branch from main)

tags.description: Work on a branch (a unit of work) rather than the protected main branch: fork a branch from main, list your branches, and read / validate / apply deltas against a specific branch.
tags.name: branches

tags.description: Read the canonical graph for a project, validate a delta batch (dry-run), or apply a delta batch through the OCC + idempotency middleware chain.
tags.name: graph

tags.description: Liveness probes. Unauthenticated. Used by load balancers and uptime monitors.
tags.name: health

tags.description: List the projects your principal can see. Filtered by membership + (for API keys) the per-key project allowlist.
tags.name: projects
